description: Implement the block-search pricing rule that selects the next entering arc.
commit: feat: add block-search pricing for entering arc selection
steps: Create pricing.go in the repo root (SPDX BSL-1.0, package mcf).

Implement method `func (s *solverState) findEnteringArc() (arc int, ok bool)`. Scan one block of s.blockSize non-tree arcs starting at s.nextArc, wrapping around the full arc space [0, s.m). Within the scanned block, track the single arc with the maximum `violation` (using s.violation from 03b); only an arc with violation > 0 is eligible. If a positive-violation arc is found in the current block, return it (update s.nextArc to one past the scanned block, modulo s.m, so the next call resumes from there). If the current block has no eligible arc, advance to the next block and keep scanning; stop after at most ceil(s.m / s.blockSize) blocks (one full pass). If a full pass produces no eligible arc, return (0, false) to signal optimality. Always update s.nextArc before returning so subsequent calls rotate.
Tree arcs (state == stateTree) yield violation 0 and are naturally skipped.

Create pricing_test.go. Construct a solverState by hand with m=8, blockSize=3, state = [stateLower x8], and hand-set cost/pi/source/target so that arc indices have reduced costs [+5, -2, +1, -7, +3, -4, 0, -1]. Under stateLower, violations are [-5, +2, -1, +7, -3, +4, 0, +1]. TestFindEnteringArcSelectsMaxViolationInBlock: first call with nextArc=0, blockSize=3 scans arcs 0,1,2 -> best eligible is arc 1 (violation 2). Assert findEnteringArc returns (1, true) and s.nextArc == 3. TestFindEnteringArcRotates: second call from nextArc=3 with blockSize=3 scans arcs 3,4,5 -> best is arc 3 (violation 7). Assert returns (3, true). TestFindEnteringArcReturnsFalseWhenOptimal: flip all states to stateTree and assert findEnteringArc returns (0, false).
acceptance_criteria: findEnteringArc returns the max-violation eligible arc within one block when one exists, otherwise scans additional blocks until it finds one or completes a full pass.
s.nextArc rotates monotonically (mod s.m) so no block starves.
When no arc is eligible across a full pass, returns (0, false).
